def: s.f. = S باهليه बाहुल्य bāhulya, s.m. Abundance, plenty, quantity, multitude, multiplicity, manifoldness, variety.
v.n. To revolve, &c. = bhamnā, q.v.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀯𑀳𑀤𑀺 (vahadi), from Sanskrit वहति (váhati).

def: v.t. To cause to return, to bring back, bring again.
adv. Again, a second time.
s.f. Son's wife, daughter-in-law.
v.n. To come again, come back, return.
adv. = bahuri, q.v.

s.m. & adv. Much, most, many; in a great degree; abundant, plentiful; enough; too much, excessive, enormous; — abundance, plenty, a great deal; sufficiency, enough; — very, very much, largely, considerably; extremely, exceedingly, excessively; immoderately, immeasurably; sadly, sorely, grievously; marvellously; notably; singularly; — three (in counting; see bahutā)
subs: bahut běhtar, bahut ḵẖūb, bahut-mubārak, adv. Very good, very well
s.m. A good deal, a great deal, plenty
adv. For the most part, mostly, frequently, generally; at the most
To become more intimate than formerly.
ex: بس یہ بہت ہیں میرے لیے۔
bas ye bahut ha͠i mere liye.
That's it, this is enough for me.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀩𑀳𑀼𑀢𑁆𑀢 (bahutta), from Sanskrit बहुत्व (bahutva, “muchness, abundance, multitude”), from Sanskrit बहु (bahu, “much, many, abundant”) from Proto-Indo-European *bʰenǵʰ- (“thick”). The development from a noun to an adjective was influenced by Prakrit 𑀅𑀳𑀼𑀢𑁆𑀢 (pahutta-), from Sanskrit प्रभूत (prabhūta, “come forth; abundant”). Compare Bengali বহুত (bohut), Gujarati બહુ (bahu…

def: adj. = bahut, q.v.
s.f. Plenty, &c. = bahutāt, q.v.; — adj. (from bahut), Three (commonly used by weighers of grain, &c. when they come to the third ser, &c.).
s.m. An ornament worn on the upper arm, a kind of armlet.
s.f. = bahutāt, q.v.
Muchness, abundance, plentitude, plenty, excess, multitude, majority (syn. kas̤rat; afrāṭ).
s.f. = bahutāt, q.v.
adj. & adv. Many, very many, most; much, very much, ever so much; abundant; greatly; for the greater part, mostly.
adj. & adv. Many, very many, most; much, very much, ever so much; abundant; greatly; for the greater part, mostly.
adv. In many ways or manners; in many places.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بِهْتَر (bihtar). False cognate of English better.

def: s.m. = bahutā; bahutāt; qq.v.
v.t. = bahānā, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Outer, external, outward; relating to abroad, foreign, strange; — outsider, stranger, foreigner; one of a low caste.
s.m. Flood, torrent, inundation (syn. bahāʼo, t̤ug̠yānī).
s.f. Lady, mistress, madam; woman, wife (local; cf. bāʼīkā); — dancing-girl, harlot; mother bawd, la bonne
subs: s.f. Mother bawd, the old abbess (syn. nāyikā).

def: s.f. Side of a bedstead, &c. (syn. paṭṭī).
s.f. Buying and selling, commerce; selling; sale
subs: s.f. Foreclosure of mortgage; absolute or unconditional sale
s.f. Conditional sale or mortgage; contract of pawn or usufruct; — baiʻ biʼl wafā-dār, s.m. A person having the possession and usufruct of a property on its conditional sale to him
s.m. Lease or sub-lease obtained by purchase
s.f. Sale by order of government
s.f. Sale which is null and void
s.m. Deed of sale; bill of sale; certificate of sale ( = qabāla-ě-nīlāmī)
s.m. Buying and selling; sale and purchase; transaction (syn. ḵẖarīd-o-faroḵẖt).

ety: S. वायु; cf. bāʼo = S. वात
def: s.f. Wind, air; flatulency; rheumatism; delirium, raving
subs: bai-birang, bā-birang, bābrang (S. vāyu+viḍanga), s.m. A medicinal seed, Embelia ribes, employed to remove flatulency or rheumatism ( = bāʼo-birang, q.v.): bāʼī paćnā, v.n. To be mortified; to be depressed; to die away
v.n. To break wind ( = bāʼo sarnā)
v.n. To prate, talk foolishly; to rave in delirium (N.B. — For compounds not found in this place, see s.v. bāʼo).

def: s.m. Earnest-money; advance
subs: (-ko), To give earnest-money, give money in advance, to bind a bargain.

def: s.f. The act of promising or swearing allegiance and obedience; submission, obedience, allegiance, homage, fealty; initiation as a disciple of a saint or religious guide
subs: (-kī), To take the oath of allegiance, &c.; to become a disciple of some saint.

def: adv. At a distance, afar off.
s.f. The Bible.
s.m. Power, wealth, grandeur, greatness, pre-eminence.
adj. Far, far off, distant, remote
subs: baʻīd aql-se, adj. Far removed from reason, unreasonable, improbable, foolish, absurd (syn. budh-biparīt)
adj. & s.m. Far from home; — a foreigner.

def: s.m. Man of the medical caste, physician.
s.f. Practice of physic.
s.f. The practice or science of physic ( = baidāʼī).
s.m. The pawn at chess.
adj. & s.m. Acquainted with or versed in the Vedas; — a reader of the Vedas; a Brāhmaṇ well versed in the Vedas.
s.m. = baidak, q.v.
adv. In abundance, plentifully, copiously ( = ifrāt-se).
s.m. The egg-plant = baiṅgan, q.v.
s.f. A sort of wood.
s.f. Rheumatism ( = bāʼī, q.v.).
s.f. Flag, standard, banner; the standard of Vishnu
subs: baijantī-mālā, s.f. A kind of garland; the necklace of Vishnu (composed of five gems produced from the five elements of nature, viz. the sapphire, from the earth; the pearl, from water; the ruby, from fire; the topaz, from air; and the diamond, from space or æther).

def: s.m. A species of black pulse.
s.f. Woman, wife. (The word is peculiar to Western India: — the Marahṭī form is बायकी).
s.m. Afternoon, evening ( = bikāl).
s.f. Woman, wife. (The word is peculiar to Western India: — the Marahṭī form is बायकी).
s.m. A name of Vishnu; the paradise or heaven of Vishnu; Paradise (syn. bihisht)
subs: s.m. Dweller of Paradise; deceased; — baikuṇṭh-bāshī honā or ho-jānā, v.n. To become a dweller in Paradise, to go to heaven; to die.

def: s.m. Bullock, ox; (Astron.) Taurus; (met.) Blockhead, fool
subs: s.m. Bullocks impressed for the public service

def: adj. Barren (a cow).
s.m. A species of bird.
s.f. = bahlī, q.v.
adv. By reason (of), on the ground (of), on account (of).
corr. of be-īmān, q.v.s.v. be.
s.m. = bemāt-bhāʼī, q.v.s.v. bemāt.
s.f. A large well ( = bāʼolī, the common word; — bāʼīṅ and bāwī would appear to be Dakkhinī words).
adj. Left = bāṅyāṅ, q.v., baiṅ-hatthā, adj. Left-handed.
prep. Separation, interval, distance between; — between, among
subs: adj. Betwixt and between, intermediate (between good and evil, &c.); middling, mediocre, passable, tolerable, indifferent; —

def: s.m. Sound, word, speech, saying ( = bānī); weeping, lamenting (for the dead); lamentation, wailing.
s.m. Sweetmeat, cakes, &c. distributed at marriages and other ceremonies, &c. ( = bāʼenā, q.v.).
s.m. An ornament (consisting of a small circular plate of gold with enamel work) worn by women on the forehead.
s.m. Mark placed on the forehead by Hindūs preparatory to devotion (syn. tilak; ṭiklī; cf. bain, baiṅdī; būṅdā).
s.f. Throwing up water with a basket from a pond or reservoir for the purpose of irrigation.
s.f. An ornamental circlet made with a coloured earth or unguent on the forehead and between the eyebrows; an ornament or fillet worn by women on the forehead ( = bain, q.v.).
s.m. The egg-plant, Solanum melongena, and its fruit, brinjal ( = baigan).
adj. Of the coloar of baiṅgan, purple ( = baiṅjnī).
s.f. = bahangī, q.v
s.f. A basket in which snakes are carried about for show.
adj. Purple = baiṅgnī, q.v.
s.m. A subdivision of the Gūjar tribe.
s.m. = benṭ.
s.m. Traffic, trade, merchandise; business, labour
s.m. Trader, merchant.
s.m. Enmity, hostility, animosity, ill-will, hatred, revenge, malice, rancour
subs: bair bisānā, bair rakhnā, bair karnā (-se), To be at enmity (with); to bear a grudge (against), to bear malice, to hate
s.m. Provoker of enmity; bearer of malice, one who owes a grudge; enemy
s.m. Hostile relations; (wife's) natural enemies (viz. the saut or co-wife; the nand or husband's sister; the sās or mother-in-law; the sisters-in-law; and the other relations)
bair nikālnā (apnā), To take revenge, avenge oneself, to retaliate
bair-yukt, adj. Inimical, hostile, revengeful, malicious.

def: s.m. Bearer; valet.
adj. With the intention or design, intending.
s.m. Freedom from passion or from worldly attachments, subjection of the appetites; renunciation of worldly pleasures, seclusion from the world; devotion, austerity, penance
subs: To withdraw from the world, renounce the pleasures of the world; to lead the life of an ascetic; to become a recluse in consequence of unrequited love.

def: s.m. = H بيراگن बैरागन bairāgan, s.f. A small cross-shaped stick or piece of iron which a Bairāgī places under his arm-pit to lean upon as he sits.
s.f. = H بيراگا बैरागा bairāgā, s.m. A small cross-shaped stick or piece of iron which a Bairāgī places under his arm-pit to lean upon as he sits.
s.f. The wife of a bairāgī; a female bairāgī.

adj. & s.m. Separated from passion or worldly attachments; austere, recluse; — one who has subdued his worldly desires and passions, one who abandons the pursuits of this world; ascetic, devotee, stoic, recluse; a class of Hindū faqīrs who roam the country and practise certain austerities.
s.f. The wife of a bairāgī; a female bairāgī.
s.m. bairāg, q.v.
s.m. = bairaq, q.v.
s.m. Flag, standard, banner, pennant, ensign; flag set up on taking possession of new or unoccupied land; (hence) taking possession of such land, or official authority to do so.
s.m. Flag, standard, banner, pennant, ensign; flag set up on taking possession of new or unoccupied land; (hence) taking possession of such land, or official authority to do so.
s.m. Flag, standard, banner, pennant, ensign; flag set up on taking possession of new or unoccupied land; (hence) taking possession of such land, or official authority to do so.
s.f. See s.v. bair.
s.f. Female enemy (see bairī).
adj. Bearing (postage), unpaid.
s.m. Enemy, foe, adversary.
s.f. Hawk, falcon ( = bahrī, q.v.).
s.m. (f. ?), Occasion, cause, reason, motive, incentive; subject, author; — postposn. Because (of, -ke), by reason (of); on account (of), by means (of).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian باعث (bā'is), from Arabic بَاعِث (bāʕiṯ).

def: adj. Twenty-two.
s.f. Energy, strength, vigour; time of health and strength, youth, prime of life; age, time of life, stage of existence.
s.m. The third of the four Hindū castes; a man of that caste (whose chief duties are agriculture and trade; hence) agriculturist; trader; — name of a tribe of Rājpūts
subs: (S. vaiśyā+vāṭika), s.m. The residence of Bais (a prince of the bais tribe); the nativity of Bais. (The province of Baiswāṛā is situated in Oudh, and is so named from a tribe of Rājpūts named Bais who formerly resided there. A prince of the tribe formerly reigned at Dunḍiyā-khera, and his dominions extended over a great part of the province of Oudh, on the north bank of the Ganges).

def: s.m. = bais, q.v.
part. adj. Seated; settled, &c. = baiṭhā, q.v.
s.f. A female of the bais caste.
s.f. (prov.), Sitting, seat; — a spot in a jungle to which cattle are sent out to graze, grazing ground; — adj. & s.m.f. (prov.), Old, worn out; ill, sick; — old and worn out animal.
s.m. The first of the twelve months constituting the Hindū solar year (answering to April-May).
s.m. = baisākh, q.v.
s.m. A club consecrated to the month Baisākh; a club bound with iron; a crutch (syn. bairāgā).

adj. & s.f. Of or relating to the month Baisākh; growing in Baisākh; — the day of full moon in the month of Baisākh; the crops of Baisākh; — a large kind of myrobalan or citron; — a club; a crutch ( = baisākhā).
s.m. Epithet of Agni or fire; fire.
(from baisnā), adj. m.f. Sitting still, sedentary, inactive, idle, lazy.
s.f. A female of the bais caste.
s.f. = bais, q.v.
s.m. Hermit, anchoret.

adj. & s.m. Of or relating to Vishnu; — a follower of Vishnu; a kind of vagrant mendicant (who bears the marks and insignia of Vishnu, and recites hymns in honour of his avatārs, especially in the forms of Rāma and Kr̤ishṇa)
subs: (vaishnava+uttara), s.m. Lands granted rent free to the worshippers of Vishnu (especially to those of the mendicant order).

def: s.m. Follower of Vishnu.
s.f. The royal army (so called because composed of the troops of the twenty-two ṣūbas or provinces of the then Mogul empire); — a command of twenty-two thousand men
subs: (-par), To attack with one's whole force.

def: v.t. = baiṭhānā, q.v.
v.n. (prov.) To sit down, &c. = baiṭhnā, q.v.; — also = paiṭhnā, q.v.
s.f. A female of the bais caste.
s.m. See s.v. bais.
s.f. Abode, house, temple, edifice; couplet, distich, verse (in poetry)
subs: s.m. lit. 'The house of God'; the temple of Mecca
s.m. lit. 'The sacred house'; the temple of Mecca ( = baituʼl-lāh)
s.m. The mansion of eminence; the highest mansion (of a planet, &c.)

s.m. Idol temple: baituʼl-māl, s.m. Public treasury or exchequer; escheat, property that falls to the crown through failure of heirs; the effects of one who dies intestate; confiscated property; (met.), an unfortunate fellow
bait-i-maʻmūr, s.m. A house in heaven, or in the sky, over against the qaʻba of Mecca (as believed by the Moḥammadans)
s.m. The temple or city of Jerusalem

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Unlucky, unfortunate; vicious; — unlucky wight, &c.
adj. Stolen
s.m. One who practises the veterinary art, horse-doctor, farrier.
s.f. Dry ginger (syn. soṅṭh).
s.f. Name of a river which (according to the Hindūs) is to be crossed by the dead on their way into the world of spirits (like the Styx of the ancients); name of a river in Orissa.
s.f. Value of the government share of produce; amount settled on the land.
part. adj. Seated, sitting; settled, settled down; fallen in (as a roof, &c.); — s.m. Paddle (for rowing a boat)
subs: v.n. To remain sitting, to sit; to give up (attempting a thing), to desist
adv. Sitting quietly; while comfortably seated: in a state of ease and comfort; without any trouble, labour, or exertion; easily
adv. = baiṭhe-biṭhāʼe, q.v.; (also) through continued sitting idle or unoccupied, through inactivity

def: s.f. Seating; settling; allaying, &c.
s.m. Chamber, hall, or house where meetings are held, assembly-room, house of relaxation, club-house; — baiṭhak denā, v.t. To make an offering at a temple or mosque in fulfilment of a vow.

v.t. To cause to sit down, to cause to be seated, to seat; to cause to settle down, to settle, establish; to found; to station, fix, place; to apply; to set, plant; to calm, becalm, allay, assuage; to press down, make flat or even (also written biṭhānā; biṭhlanā, q.v.)

v.t. To cause to sit down, to cause to be seated, to seat; to cause to settle down, to settle, establish; to found; to station, fix, place; to apply; to set, plant; to calm, becalm, allay, assuage; to press down, make flat or even (also written biṭhānā; biṭhlanā, q.v.)
s.m. Allocation, situation, position.

v.t. To cause to sit down, to cause to be seated, to seat; to cause to settle down, to settle, establish; to found; to station, fix, place; to apply; to set, plant; to calm, becalm, allay, assuage; to press down, make flat or even (also written biṭhānā; biṭhlanā, q.v.)
s.m. Chamber, hall, or house where meetings are held, assembly-room, house of relaxation, club-house; — baiṭhak denā, v.t. To make an offering at a temple or mosque in fulfilment of a vow.
s.f. A kind of gymnastic exercise ( = baiṭhkā, q.v.).
v.t. = baiṭhana, baiṭhālna, q.v.

v.n. To seat oneself, sit down, be seated, be unemployed or idle; to sit, brood, incubate; to alight, settle, perch; to ride (on, -par); to subside, settle, abate; to pair, couple (a bird); to obtain employment or service; to be laid out or expended (on, -par); to visit a person in grief for the purpose of condolence; to sit in dharnā (q.v.)
subs: v.n. To sit down; to settle down; to fall in or down (a house, wall, &c.).

def: adj. Having a flat bottom, flat.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Unlucky, unfortunate; vicious; — unlucky wight, &c.
adv. With the consent (of, -ke), in agreement or unison (with); with one consent, concurrently, conjointly, harmoniously, unanimously ( = biʼl-ittifāq, q.v.).
adj. & s.m. Of or relating to marriage, matrimonial, nuptial; — wedding; the parent of a daughter's husband, or of a son's wife
s.m. Revolution; change or modification of existence.
s.f. Legal opinion, judgment or decree according to Hindū law (syn. fatwā).
adv. In place (of), in lieu (of), instead.
s.m. The weaver bird ( = bayā, q.v.).
s.m. Grammarian.
s.m. Explanation, de scription, interpretation.
s.f. A woman (in general)
subs: s.f. Woman, wife ( = bīr-bānī, q.v.s.v. bīr).

def: adj. Clear, lucid, evident, manifest.
s.m. A mark fixed to public writings by the magistrate or any principal officer.
s.m. Egg; testicle.

ety: fem. of abyaẓ; rt. بيض 'to surpass in whiteness'
def: adj. & s.m. White; bright, clear, clean, pure; — the sun
subs: To distinguish oneself in any work; to do wonders, perform a miracle (in allusion to the white hand of Moses).

def: s.m. The perquisite of the magistrate, &c. for marking public papers (see baiẓ).
adj. Fashioned in the shape of an egg, egg-shaped, oval (syn. anḍākār); relating to eggs; made of eggs.
s.m.f. (?), Tribute, tax, toll, duty, impost, cess
subs: bāj-gīr, s.m. Collector of tribute or revenue; tax-gatherer
bāj-guzār, s.m. A tributary (king, &c.); feudatory or dependent state; one who pays taxes, duties, &c.

def: prep. Without, in the absence of ( = ba-juz. — The word is used by the poet Walī, and would appear to be peculiar to Southern India).
s.m. Horse ( = bājī, q.v.).
s.m. Hawk ( = bāz, of which it is a corruption).
s.m. Musical instrument; instrumental music, music
subs: To play on a musical instrument; to strike up (music)
bājā-bajantar, s.m. Band of musicians, or of music ( = bajantarī)
s.m. Various musical in struments; sound or clanguor of many and various musical instruments (also bāj-gāj)
s.m. Maker or vendor of musical instruments; player on any instrument of music, musician ( = bājantarī).
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀯𑀚𑁆𑀚 (vajja), from Sanskrit वाद्य (vādya), from Proto-Indo-Aryan *wā́dyam, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *wā́dyam.

def: adj. In place; proper, suitable, fit, becoming; right, just, true; — adv. Fitly, properly, rightly, as it should be all right; just so, precisely; (ironically) just so, very true, very like a whale; — ba-jāʼe, prep. In place (of, -ke), in lieu of, instead of
subs: s.f. Performance, accomplishment, fulfilment, execution, discharge
v.t. To perform, accomplish, fulfil, execute, discharge, carry out (an order, &c.), give effect to; to comply with, obey ( = bajānā).

def: v.t. See s.v. ba-jā.
adv. By force or violence, forcibly, violently ( = biʼl-jabr, and jabr-se).
prep. See s.v. ba-jā.
s.m. A species of serpent.
adj. Still, quiet; lying still (through fear or fatigue); crouching, contracting oneself; delaying.
adv. With (one's) whole soul, heartily, cheerfully; — in or to the soul; at or to the point of death; in great straits, in great distress, in the utmost perplexity
subs: v.n. To be in imminent danger; to be in the utmost distress or perplexity; to be greatly harassed, plagued, annoyed, vexed, &c.
v.t. To worry to death, to plague, harass, annoy exceedingly, worry, tease, vex, disgust.

def: s.m. Instruments of music

ety: S. वादयत, rt. वद्, or वाद्य+H. caus. aff. ānā
def: v.t. To cause to sound, to sound; to play on (a musical instrument), to perform or execute music; to beat (a drum, gong, &c.); to cause to ring or resound; to try the ring of (a vessel, coin, &c. in order to ascertain its soundness); to put in action, set agoing, ply (e.g. jūte bajānā)
subs: s.m. Player on a musical instrument, musician; minstrel (syn. bājantrī).

def: v.t. To execute, &c. = ba-jā lānā, q.v.
s.m. Musician, minstrel ( = bājantrī, q.v.).
s.m. Musician, &c. = bājantrī, q.v.
subs: s.m. The quarter inhabited by musicians; a brothel.

def: s.m. Musician; band of musicians; — s.f. A tax exacted (under Moḥammadan rule) from musicians, professional singers, and dancing girls
subs: s.m. The quarter of a town inhabited by musicians.

def: s.m. Musician, &c. = bājantrī, q.v.
subs: s.m. The quarter inhabited by musicians; a brothel.

def: adj. Having a projecting navel; afflicted with a disease of the navel; having a projecting belly; pot-bellied.
s.m. corr. of bāzār, q.v.
s.m. See s.v. bajr.
s.m. = bājantrī, q.v.
s.m. = bājantrī, q.v.
adj. (f. -ī), Boiled; fermented; putrefied.

ety: redupl. of baj-, rt. of bajānā; arising out of the sound given forth by a boiling or fermenting liquid, &c.
def: v.n. To simmer, bubble, boil; to effervesce; to ferment; to putrefy.
s.m. = bājā-gājā, q.v.s.v. bājā.
s.m. Marshy soil, quagmire, bog.
(caus. of bajhnā, q.v.), v t. To entangle, inveigle; to ensnare (as game), entrap, catch.
v.t. = bajhānā.

ety: bajh˚ = Prk. बज्झ(इ) = S. बध्य(ते), pass. of rt. बंध्
def: v.n. To be entangled, ensnared, entrapped, caught (syn. phaṅsnā); to be stuck, to stick (in, -meṅ)
s.m. Stalk without ear, bare stalk.
s.f. Corr. of bāzī, q.v.
s.m. Horse.
s.f. Elder sister.
adj. In earnest; pressing, importunate, persistent, obstinate, determined; — ba-jid ho-ke, adv. Earnestly; importunately, &c.
subs: v.n. To be in earnest; to press or urge (one) earnestly or vigorously; to be importunate; to be obstinate or determined; to insist; to persist.

def: s.m. An agricultural servant (in Ruhelkhanḍ) who takes corn as a recompense for his labour (in contradistinction to a měh-dār, or one who receives money).
prep. On account of; for the reason (that), because of; for the sake of.
adj. & adv. In kind, in detail; in some sort; particularly; by kind; — ba-jinsi-hi, adj. & adv. Exactly the same, the very identical; exactly as it is; intact, entirely, bodily.
s.m. Cutting open, lancing; piercing, spearing; swelling to the point of bursting (as cattle through over-eating).
s.m. corr. of bazzāz, q.v.

ety: bāj˚ = Prk. वज्ज(इ) = S. वाद्य(ते), pass. of the caus. of rt. वद्
def: v.n. To sound (as a musical instrument, &c.); to strike (as a clock); to ring, resound ( = bajnā); to be published; to be known, be notorious or famous (as); — v.t. To sound, play upon, &c. = bajānā, q.v.
s.m. A child's toy, rattle; — a rupee (used among brokers).

v.n. To sound; to strike (as a clock, &c.); to be sounded; to be struck (as a gong, &c.), be played upon (as a musical instrument), be rung (as a bell), be fired (as a gun); to chatter (as the teeth); to ring, resound, be famous or celebrated; to be busy or active (as a sword, stick, &c.); to be generally known or called (by, -se, a name, &c.), to go by the name (of).
s.f. Quarrel, fight, row, uproar.
(rel. to bajānā), v.t. lit. 'To cause to ring or resound by beating' to beat severely; to give (one) a good hiding or dressing.

adj. Adamantine, hard, impenetrable; massive, ponderous, heavy, unwieldy; heavy of movement, slow; severe, difficult, hard; shaped like a (St. Andrew's, or multiplication) cross; — s.m. The thunderbolt and weapon of Indra, thunderbolt, lightning; adamant, diamond; a hard, heavy stone
subs: (S. vajrāng, i.e. vajra+anga), bajr-angī, adj. & s.m. Having a hardy frame; — a title of Hanumān; a robust, hardy man
(S. vajra+aṅkita), adj. & s.f. Marked with a cross-shaped symbol; — a cross-shaped tilak or mark made with red lead on a Hindū's forehead
(S. vajra+āghata), s.m. Stroke of a thunderbolt; stroke of lightning; any sudden shock or calamity

(S. vajra+sphaṭ+uka), s.m. The umbrella-bearing palm, Corypha umbraculifera; the seeds of the same palm (which are used as beads for necklaces by certain sects of Hindūs: 'The tree flowers but once and dies, and the natives firmly believe that the bursting of the spadix is accompanied by a loud explosion'; whence the name)
(-par), Lightning to fall (upon), to be struck by a thunderbolt, or by lightning; to be withered, be blasted, be stricken with calamity (used in cursing, e.g. bajr paṛe uspar)
s.m. Name of one of the metres of Hindī poetry
s.m. lit. 'Hard reptile'; the scaly ant-eater, the Pangolin or Manis: a kind of penetrating insect which bores holes in wood and stone.

def: s.m. A species of panic or millit, panicum spicatum (it is, par excellence, the 'food' of the poorer classes)
subs: 'To be scattered like bājrā'; to be restless, uneasy, disturbed; to be weary or tired
s.m. A speckled fowl (particularly a black fowl with yellow spots of the size of a grain of bājrā).

def: s.m. A species of panic or millit, panicum spicatum (it is, par excellence, the 'food' of the poorer classes)
subs: 'To be scattered like bājrā'; to be restless, uneasy, disturbed; to be weary or tired
s.m. A speckled fowl (particularly a black fowl with yellow spots of the size of a grain of bājrā).

def: s.m. A kind of boat commonly used for travelling; a large kind of pleasure-boat (round-bottomed and keel-less, — commonly called 'budgerow').
s.m. = bajrā, q.v.
adj. & s.m. See s.v. bajr.
s.f. A kind of millet smaller than bājrā.
s.f. Gravel; small hailstone (syn. olā).
s.m. Arm; ornament worn on the arm, armlet; fold of a door; door-post, side-frame of a door, &c. (see bāzū)
s.m. An ornament worn on the upper arm; armlet.
prep. With the exception (of, -ke), excepting, except, besides.
v.t. To cause to sound; to get (one) to play (on an instrument); to have (a drum, &c.) sounded.

ety: old form bāyaka; rt. Zend bī = S. भी
def: Fear, apprehension.

ety: S. वाक्य, rt. वच्; Prk. वक्क
def: s.m. Word, speech, language, dialect; saying, proverb, maxim; (in Gram.) sentence; — verbal estimate (without actual measurement) of the produce of a field.
s.m. = bākh, q.v.
s.m. The heron, Ardea torra, or A. Nivea (syn. baglā)
subs: s.f. 'Heron's walk,' slow walk, strut ( = bag-ćāl)

ety: rt. of bak-nā; S. वाक्, rt. वच्
def: s.f. Prattle, prate, babble, chatter, silly talk, twaddle, nonsense, raving
subs: bak-jhak karnā, v.n. To prattle, prate, chatter, jabber, &c.
v.n. To be talkative or garrulous, to prate, &c. ( = bak-bak karnā); to rave (in delirium), be delirious
s.f. Talkativeness, garrulity; raving, delirium; — bak-wād, s.f. Idle or nonsensical talk ( = bakwās)
s.m. One who talks nonsense, idle or foolish talker.

def: s.f. Remaining; duration, permanence; eternity; immortality.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بَقَا (baqā), borrowed from Arabic بَقَاء (baqāʔ).

def: adv. By the power (of), by means (of); by or in the quantity (of); to the extent or measure (of).
adv. In or at the back or rear; behind.
adv. In the hand; in hand ( = ba-dast).
adv. With the limitation or restriction (of), restricted by; with the condition; along with.
s.f. Cow (or other animal) whose milk has diminished.
s.m. Beau, fop. &c. = bāṅkait; bāṅkā, q.q.v.
s.f. Sappan wood, Cœsalpinia sappan.
adv. According to desire, to (one's) desire or object; agreeable to (one's) wish.
s.f. = bakāyan, q.v.
s.m. (?), Portion of the crop (about two-fifths), paid as rent to the cultivators by the zamīndārs.
s.m. Account or value of a crop fixed by an appraiser by word of mouth.
adj. & adv. In use; for use; of use, useful, serviceable
subs: v.n. To be of use; come into use.

def: s.m.f. Ox, bull; cow
subs: s.m. A festival held, by Moḥammadans, on the tenth of the month ẕī-ḥijja, in commemoration of Abraham's readiness to sacrifice his son (Isaac, according to the Shīʻas, but Ishmael, according to the Mohammadans of the Sunnī sect. The Qorʼān, it may be observed, does not mention the name of the son

def: s.m. Intelligence forwarded orally; messenger, courier, fore-runner, harbinger; letter; invoice; answer (from a person possessed by an evil spirit)
subs: (-ko), To give an answer, to answer.

def: s.m. Ring worn on the second toe.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit बर्कर (barkara, “kid, lamb”) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-).

def: s.f. Virginity; maiden head ( = bikr, q.v.).
v.t. To answer, &c. ( = bakārā denā, q.v.s.v.bakārā).

ety: per met. for bāsak = S. वासक
def: s.m. The shrub Justicia ganderussa ( = bāsā: It is used to make charcoal for the manufacture of gunpowder).
adv. By oath; on oath.
adv. In abundance, plentifully.
corr. of وقت waqt, q.v.
adv. According to the saying or dictum (of), according to.
s.m. Head cook, superintendent of the kitchen, steward, butler; cupbearer.
adj. & s.f. Relating to a bakāwal; — cooking materials.
s.f. Wife of a bakāwal; a female bakāwal.
s.m. Remainders, remains, balances, arrears, dues; balance of revenue.
s.f. Persian Lilac; Bastard Cedar; common bead-tree, Melia azadiracht, or M. sempervirens.
adj. Astringent = kaselā, q.v.
v.n. To prate, &c. = bak-bak karnā, q.v.s.v. bak.
s.f. The plant Vernonia anthelmintica (the seed of which is used for curing the itch. It is also called Serratula, and Conyza, anthelmintica).
s.m. Twine made from the root of the ḍhāk tree.
s.m. Root of a grass (mūnj, &c.) of which rope is made.
s.m. Udder (of a cow, &c.).
s.m. World, universe.
s.m. Tortoise; turtle.
s.m. Grass kept for pasturage.
= bāḵẖa, q.v.
adv. Attended with good, in welfare, well, in safety; for good, to a good end or purpose; in peace and safety, alone and unmeddled with
subs: adv. Safe and sound, well, in safety.

def: s.m. = bākhar, q.v. (Dialectic forms are bakkhal, bakhrī).

ety: S. व्याख्यानं, or विख्यान, Prk. वक्खाणं
def: s.m. Explanation, exposition; definition, description, declaration, account, relation; gloss, comment, interpretation; naming, calling; praise, commendation
subs: (-kā), To expound, explain, define, describe, declare, relate; to praise, commend ( = bakhānnā).

def: s.f. = bakhān, q.v.
v.t. To expound, &c. = bakhān karnā, q.v.s.v. bakhān
subs: (-ko), To name with opprobrium, to vilify, abuse (used by women).

ety: S. वल्कलं? rt. वल्; Prk. वक्कलं
def: s.m. House; collection of houses in one enclosure; a cluster of cattle-sheds; enclosure, area, court-yard; — a bag or wallet (for tools, — as a goldsmith's); — a kind of drug used as a ferment.
s.m. A kind of bullock hoe or plough (much used in Bundelkhand to remove the hard crust off fields and to clear away surface weeds).
s.m. A kind of hoe or plough (bākhar, q.v.).
s.m. = H بکهاري बखारी bakhārī, s.f. Storehouse, granary (syn. khātā, bhanḍār).
s.f. = H بکهار बखार bakhār, s.m. Storehouse, granary (syn. khātā, bhanḍār).

ety: S. विकिर; or v.n. fr. next
def: s.f. Scattering, &c. (see next); scattered portion, fallen fragment, anything dropped.

ety: S. वि+कार, or कृत, rt. कृ+कः
def: s.m. Entanglement, complication, imbroglio, difficulty, hitch: any troublesome or vexatious business, affair or occurrence; trouble, care, trial, worry, agitation; obstacle, impediment, encumbrance, clog; (hence) goods, chattels, furniture; lumber, rubbish; disagreement, difference, dispute, contention, broil, wrangling, altercation, quarrel, brawl, disturbance, pother, tumult, bustle, uproar; — clever contrivance, dodge, resource, artifice
subs: (-kā), To settle a dispute; to finish a troublesome business
bakheṛā karnā, bakheṛā maćānā (-meṅ), To complicate, make difficult or troublesome, throw obstacles (in the way of), raise or create a difficulty; to bring about a quarrel, &c., cause contention.

def: part. Scattered, &c. (see bakhernā); — adj. Quarrelsome, &c. = bakheṛiyā.
adj. & s.m. Quarrelsome, contentious, captious; — quarrelsome person, wrangler, brawler; mischief-maker, firebrand; rogue, knave, astute rascal.
adj. & s.m. Miserly, avaricious, stingy; — miser, niggard (syn. kanjūs).
adv. In opposition (to, -ke), contrary (to); on the contrary.
s.f. Niggardliness, &c. = buḵẖl, q.v. (syn. kanjūsī).
s.m. = بخيه baḵẖya, q.v.
s.m. = بخيه baḵẖya, q.v.
v.t. = baḵẖyānā, q.v.
v.t. To stitch, &c. = baḵẖya-karnā, q.v.s.v. baḵẖya.
s.f. = bākhṛī, q.v.
s.m. Share, portion, lot; allotment, distribution, dividend; allowance
subs: (-kā), To allot, distribute; to allow, assign.

def: s.m. Share, portion, lot; allotment, distribution, dividend; allowance
subs: (-kā), To allot, distribute; to allow, assign.

def: s.m. Partner, sharer; shareholder.

(i.e. bākh, q.v.+dim. aff. ṛī), s.f. lit. 'A young cow whose udder is developed'; a cow or buffalo gone about five months with calf; or which has calved about five months ago; (locally) a young cow or buffalo.
s.m. Partner, sharer; shareholder.
s.f. House, cottage, hut; round granary of grass, reeds, and mud, raised on piles.
s.m. Householder.

ety: Zend bakhsh, rt. bag; S. भक्ष, rt. भज्
def: part. adj. & s.m. Giving, imparting, bestowing; yielding; forgiving; — giver, bestower; forgiver (used as last member of compounds, e.g. tāj-baḵẖsh, 'bestowing a diadem'; s̤amar-baḵẖsh, adj. fruit-yielding
subs: s.m. forgiver of sin); — share, lot, portion; donation, gift; allowance, pay; pardon, forgiveness

def: s.f. Favour, bounty, present; forgiveness, pardon.
s.m. Giving, bestowing; forgiving
v.t. To cause to give; to obtain forgiveness or pardon (for another); to have (one's own) sin or offence pardoned or excused ( = baḵẖshwānā).

ety: act. part. of baḵẖshīdan; and = baḵẖsh, q.v.+Zend añṭ, S. ant
def: adj. & s.m. Giving, bestowing; — giver, bestower, donor; pardoner, forgiver, God.
s.f. Liberality, generosity; forgiveness.
v.t. Intens. of baḵẖshnā, q.v.

s.m. Paymaster (in Moham. armies); general, commander-in-chief (the office of paymaster being combined with that of general); an officer who kept an account of all disbursements connected with military tenures (as those of manṣabdārs and jāg̠īr-dārs); — collector of house-rents
subs: s.f. Office or duty of a general or commander-in-chief; generalship.

def: s.f. Present, gift, donation, grant, gratuity, reward; beneficence, liberality, generosity; pardon, forgiveness
s.m. Paymasters, &c.
s.m.? Name of a division of the Jaunpur district.
v.t. To give, grant, bestow; to forgive, pardon, excuse.
v.t. To cause to be forgiven, to get or have pardoned or excused; to have (oneself) excused or let off from.

ety: Pehl. bakht; Zend bakhta, perf. part. rt. bag; S. bhakta, rt. bhaj
def: s.m. Portion, lot, fortune; good fortune, luck, prosperity (syn. bhāg, naṣīb)
subs: s.f. Trial of fortune or luck
adj. Fortunate, lucky; — s.m. Fortunate or lucky man
s.f. Playing with fortune, trying (one's) luck; chance
adj. Favoured of fortune, fortunate, lucky
s.m. One whose fortune is wide awake, prosperous person
(or baḵẖtoṅ) jalnā, v.n. To lose (one's) luck, become unfortunate
s.m. One whose fortune is asleep, unfortunate person
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Unfortunate, wretched; unfortunate person, wretched creature, miserable wight.

ety: of bāḵẖtan; rt. Zend baz; S. bhaj
def: Played; staked; lost, beaten (at play).
adj. See s.v. baḵẖt.
adj. = baḵẖt-war, q.v.s.v. baḵẖt.
adv. In a good manner, well, properly, duly, thoroughly, completely, in perfection.
s.m. substance for fumigation, incense, frankincense; perfume, scent, odour.
adv. To oneself; in oneself; of oneself, spontaneously, voluntarily
subs: To come to oneself, be oneself again, recover (one's) senses.
